sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_strings (id INT PRIMARY KEY, str_col VARCHAR(50), num_col INT);
INSERT INTO test_strings (id, str_col, num_col) VALUES (1, 'highigh', 5), (2, '', 10), (3, 'low', NULL);
SELECT id, charindex('gh', str_col) AS position FROM test_strings WHERE charindex('gh', str_col) <= num_col;
SELECT id, str_col, charindex('low', str_col) AS position_low FROM test_strings WHERE charindex('low', str_col) <= 10;
SELECT id, CASE WHEN charindex('gh', str_col) > 0 THEN num_col ELSE 0 END AS adjusted_num FROM test_strings WHERE str_col IS NOT NULL;
CREATE TABLE another_table (id INT, ref_id INT, CONSTRAINT fk_ref FOREIGN KEY (ref_id) REFERENCES test_strings(id));
INSERT INTO another_table (id, ref_id) VALUES (1, 1), (2, 2), (3, NULL);
SELECT a.id, s.str_col, charindex('gh', s.str_col) AS pos_gh FROM another_table a JOIN test_strings s ON a.ref_id = s.id WHERE charindex('gh', s.str_col) <= a.id;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_strings GROUP BY CASE WHEN charindex('gh', str_col) > 0 THEN 'found' ELSE 'not_found' END HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;
SELECT str_col, COUNT(*) OVER(PARTITION BY charindex('gh', str_col)) AS occurrences FROM test_strings ORDER BY occurrences DESC;
